seo.title: Free online PDF tools - PDFTool.work
seo.description: Use browser-based PDF tools for merging, splitting, compressing, rotating, unlocking, and organizing everyday documents.

lead: Handle common PDF tasks in your browser with clear limits and practical guidance.

strings.heroLead: Merge, split, compress, rotate, unlock, and organize everyday PDFs in your browser. Selected file contents are handled locally by the page; normal analytics and advertising requests may still load for the website.
strings.primaryCtaLabel: Open the PDF upload preparation tool
strings.primaryCtaTitle: Prepare a PDF against upload requirements
strings.secondaryCtaLabel: Jump to the PDF tools list
strings.proofLead: PDF results depend on the browser, device resources, and the internal structure of the document. The site may also load analytics and ads to support operation.
strings.proofBrowser: Selected PDF contents are read by the current browser for the tool operation.
strings.proofLimits: Very large files can be limited by browser memory, device performance, and PDF structure.
strings.toolsLead: Choose a tool for application packets, contracts, receipts, course material, and routine document cleanup.
strings.toolCompressText: Try to reduce file size while reminding you to review readability and page structure.
strings.toolMergeText: Combine several files into one document for applications, attachments, or archives.
strings.toolSplitText: Extract the pages you need and avoid sending extra material.
strings.toolManageText: Delete, reorder, and rotate pages to clean up scans and multi-page packets.
strings.workflowTitle: Simple, transparent, and useful for one-off tasks
strings.workflowLead: PDFTool.work is built around static pages and browser-side scripts. It does not require an account or a complicated back-office workflow.
strings.workflowStepOneText: Select a local PDF on the tool page so your browser can read it and show the needed status.
strings.workflowStepTwoText: Adjust target size, page ranges, or rotation before starting the operation.
strings.workflowStepThreeText: Results are usually created as browser download links; after a refresh you may need to choose the file again.
strings.faqLocalQuestion: Does PDFTool.work upload my PDF to its server?
strings.faqLocalAnswer: The tools are designed for the browser to read and process the selected PDF contents locally. The page itself may still request static assets, analytics scripts, or advertising resources.
strings.faqLargeQuestion: What if a large file fails?
strings.faqLargeAnswer: Use a desktop browser, close memory-heavy tabs, and check whether the PDF is a large scan or has a complex internal structure.
